Bird Conservation Data · Multiple Geographies

The Bird Conservation Atlas

This atlas maps where birds are recognized as conservation priorities — starting with State Wildlife Action Plans, State and Federal Endangered Species Act listings, and eBird abundance by state, with flyways, regions, and other levels to follow.

—bird taxa
—units mapped
—listings
Territories
Birds listed Lists this birdDoes not

About the data. Bird taxa each U.S. state or territory designated as Species of Greatest Conservation Need (SGCN) in its State Wildlife Action Plan, compiled by the USGS from USFWS records. The year shown for each state or territory is its most recent plan revision in this dataset. A state or territory "listing" a bird means that taxon appears in its SWAP.

Groups are an approximate convenience grouping derived from common names, not a formal taxonomy. Alaska, Hawaii, and the five territories are drawn to their own geometry; the Pacific and Caribbean island endemics appear in the territory tiles below the map.

About this atlas. This tool is built to grow: each geographic level — state, flyway, region, or other boundary — plugs into the same map, search, and comparison interface. Use the level switcher above to see what's available now and what's coming next.
